title: Jessie Buckley Wins Golden Globe for Hamnet - Deadline
text: Jessie Buckley won Best Actress at the Golden Globes for her role in Hamnet, directed by Chloé Zhao. The film is based on Maggie O'Farrell's novel, depicting William Shakespeare's life during his most famous work while exploring his family's tragedy. Buckley highlighted the production's diverse crew composition: we were telling the story of probably the most famous Brit that ever lived and we had a Chinese director, a lot of Irish, a mostly Polish crew. She thanked the cast and creative team, including Zhao, Paul Mescal, O'Farrell, and the child actors. She humorously acknowledged Polish key grip Tomasz Sternicki for making excellent stew on set. Buckley also praised her fellow nominees, calling Julia Roberts a hero to all actresses. Buckley became the first Irish person to win in this Golden Globes category.
